name: Oracle Textura Construction Payment Management API
what_it_does: Oracle Textura Payment Management REST API for general contractor-to-subcontractor payment management, compliance documentation collection, lien waiver management, and owner billing on commercial construction projects. Enables AI agents to manage payment application submission and routing for automated subcontractor billing workflow, handle compliance document collection and verification gated to payment for compliance-first payment automation, access lien waiver collection and conditional/unconditional routing for payment release automation, retrieve payment approval workflow status for cash flow forecasting automation, manage retainage calculation and release for construction contract financial management, handle owner billing (AIA G702 style) and approval routing for general contractor billing automation, access payment analytics and cash flow reporting for construction finance intelligence, retrieve subcontractor prequalification and insurance verification status for payment eligibility automation, manage change order impact on schedule of values for contract change management, and integrate Oracle Textura with Oracle Aconex, Oracle Fusion, and financial systems for enterprise construction payment lifecycle.
use_cases: Processing subcontractor pay apps from payment automation agents
Collecting lien waivers from compliance agents
Tracking owner billing from GC finance agents
Managing retainage from contract finance agents
not_for: Residential construction without commercial payment complexity
Full construction ERP with job costing and scheduling
best_when: A general contractor, construction manager, or owner organization wanting AI agents to automate subcontractor payment application processing, compliance document collection, lien waiver management, and owner billing on commercial construction projects within the Oracle Textura platform.
avoid_when: LIEN WAIVER LEGAL AUTHORITY FOR AUTOMATED EXECUTION: Automated unconditional lien waiver execution via Oracle Textura on behalf of subcontractors requires clear contractual and legal authority; automated waiver of mechanic's lien rights without actual payment receipt creates permanent lien rights forfeiture; require confirmed payment before automated unconditional waiver execution. COMPLIANCE DOCUMENT VERIFICATION DEPTH: Automated compliance document approval in Oracle Textura (insurance certificates, certified payroll, safety certs) verifies document submission but not document accuracy; automated payment release based on submitted-but-unreviewed compliance documents creates regulatory and contractual exposure. RETAINAGE RELEASE AUTHORIZATION: Automated retainage release via Textura on substantial completion or final completion must be authorized by project owner and GC; automated retainage disbursement without contractual milestone confirmation creates lien exposure and contract breach. JOINT CHECK PAYMENT ROUTING: Automated joint check payments via Textura to subcontractor-supplier combinations require careful routing verification; automated joint check to wrong payee creates misdirected payment and UCC Article 9 secured creditor priority disputes.

auth.notes: Oracle Textura uses OAuth 2.0 with Oracle IDCS. REST API with JSON. Austin, Texas HQ (Oracle Corporation, NYSE: ORCL). Oracle acquired Textura Corporation in 2016 for $663M. Textura founded 2003 in Deerfield, IL. Construction payment management for large commercial GCs and project owners. Operates alongside Oracle Aconex for full construction project lifecycle. Used on $500B+ in construction projects annually. Competes with Levelset (Procore) and GCPay for construction payment management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — GC platform subscription + subcontractor transaction fees
pricing.paid_tiers: GC/owner annual platform subscription; subcontractor per-project transaction fee; API access in enterprise tier
pricing.notes: Austin TX. Oracle NYSE:ORCL. Textura acquired 2016 ($663M). Founded 2003. $500B+ in construction projects managed annually. GC platform subscription + subcontractor transaction fee model.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; payment transaction API throttled for financial data integrity

known_agent_gotchas: UNCONDITIONAL LIEN WAIVER REQUIRES PAYMENT CONFIRMATION: Automated unconditional lien waiver generation and submission via Oracle Textura must only execute after confirmed payment receipt; automated unconditional waiver before actual payment permanently forfeits subcontractor lien rights; implement payment bank clearance confirmation before unconditional waiver workflow

COMPLIANCE DOCUMENT SUBMISSION VS CONTENT ACCURACY: Oracle Textura compliance document gate tracks document submission, not document accuracy; automated payment approval based on document-submitted status without content review approves payments against expired, incorrect, or non-compliant insurance and certified payroll; implement content verification step for high-value payment approvals

ORACLE IDCS TOKEN MANAGEMENT FOR AUTOMATION: Textura OAuth via Oracle IDCS requires service account with Textura application roles in IDCS tenant; automated token refresh must handle IDCS session expiry and implement proper token cache management; IDCS access policy changes can revoke automated agent access

Joint check routing verification — automated joint check payment processing via Textura for subcontractor-to-supplier scenarios must verify correct payee combination; joint check to incorrect party creates misdirected payment with UCC Article 9 secured creditor priority complications and bank recovery difficulty

Retainage release contractual milestone verification — automated retainage release trigger in Textura must be gated to verified substantial completion or final completion milestone per contract terms; automated retainage release without contractual milestone confirmation creates owner liability and lien exposure

Aconex integration timing for document handoff — Oracle Textura integration with Oracle Aconex for compliance document sync has processing delay; automated payment approval workflow must account for Textura-Aconex document sync latency before treating uploaded compliance documents as available
agent_readiness.security_notes: Construction payment management. SOC2, ISO27001, PCI-DSS. OAuth2/IDCS. US/EU/AU. Payment and lien waiver data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Construction payment. SOC2, ISO27001, PCI-DSS. OAuth2/IDCS. US/EU/AU. Construction payment and lien waiver data.
